acceptance_targets: [chapter_13_f001] Pass if, when reading the reveal span from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:51 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:61, a reader can point to at least two specific sensations or perceptual breakdowns Gavin undergoes while sensing the condition, and the passage defines the threat through those effects plus the water-table analogy rather than mainly through what the condition is not. Fail if the span still reads primarily as abstract explanation.

[chapter_13_f002] Pass if, across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:109 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:147, the reader can identify three distinct beat changes: what the students fear, what specific ordinary details Gavin cannot bear to lose, and what he decides or resolves because of that grief. Fail if adjacent paragraph clusters in that span still make the same ignorance-or-grief point in different wording.

[chapter_13_f003] Pass if none of the emotional landings at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:65, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:123, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:147 reads as a standalone thesis or maxim, and each is carried by concrete observed detail from the surrounding scene. Fail if those lines still end on compressed slogan-like phrasing that sounds more authored than thought.

[chapter_13_f004] Pass if, across the openings at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:25, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:73, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:27, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:87, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:127, no identical two-word setup such as 'He sat' or 'The wards' begins more than twice and each renewed look at the wards or Gavin's posture arrives through a distinct sensory or cognitive angle. Fail if the same reset formula still appears three or more times in those beats.

[full_award_005] Pass if the revised tower passage at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2653-2657 makes the outside feel indifferent and structurally incompatible without using water-table, groundwater, sea, or dam language. Fail if the paragraph still reads as another version of chapter 08's hydrological explanation.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, when reading the reveal span from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:51 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:61, a reader can point to at least two specific sensations or perceptual breakdowns Gavin undergoes while sensing the condition, and the passage defines the threat through those effects plus the water-table analogy rather than mainly through what the condition is not. Fail if the span still reads primarily as abstract explanation.
findings.problem: The chapter's core encounter with the thing beyond the wards keeps resolving into negation and explanatory category-talk instead of scene-level, bodily consequence. That is sanitization drift at the exact moment this novel most needs unmediated perception: the reader understands the concept, but the reveal lands as philosophy before it lands as experience.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the reveal centered on snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:51, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:53, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:55, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:59 so Gavin's sensing of the thin place produces concrete immediate distortions on the page before the explanation hardens into theory. Keep the 'condition, not creature' frame, but give him at least two specific perceptual or bodily failures in the moment and trim some of the repeated 'not X' definitions around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:51 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:53.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:109 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:147, the reader can identify three distinct beat changes: what the students fear, what specific ordinary details Gavin cannot bear to lose, and what he decides or resolves because of that grief. Fail if adjacent paragraph clusters in that span still make the same ignorance-or-grief point in different wording.
findings.problem: After the discovery lands, the back third keeps restating the same conclusion in slightly different forms: they do not know, the world is ordinary, Gavin loves it, the world goes on. The mood is strong, but the chapter plateaus instead of continuing to turn. At award level, elegy still needs fresh consequence or pressure, not repeated paraphrase of the same emotional thesis.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress and differentiate the sequence from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:109 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:147. Let one beat handle the students' ignorance, one beat handle Gavin's attachment to specific ordinary life, and one beat convert that grief into a sharper decision or changed resolve before he leaves the tower. If the walk back through the castle remains, make the return beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:131, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:137, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:143 alter his understanding or plan rather than restating the tower thesis.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if none of the emotional landings at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:65, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:123, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:147 reads as a standalone thesis or maxim, and each is carried by concrete observed detail from the surrounding scene. Fail if those lines still end on compressed slogan-like phrasing that sounds more authored than thought.
findings.problem: Several emotional landings arrive as polished, quotable finish-lines rather than Gavin-shaped close-third thought. Phrases like the war being a 'family argument in a house that was on fire' and the closing 'small and loud and precious, not knowing' sound authorially composed in a way the style bible specifically warns against: Gavin's voice should get stranger through perception, not through maxim-like sentence polish.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the cadence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:65, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:123, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:147. Keep the underlying thought, but trade aphoristic tags and balanced three-beat flourishes for plainer, slightly awkward Gavin-specific phrasing or a return to sensory detail from the immediate scene.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across the openings at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:25, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:73, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:27, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:87, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:127, no identical two-word setup such as 'He sat' or 'The wards' begins more than twice and each renewed look at the wards or Gavin's posture arrives through a distinct sensory or cognitive angle. Fail if the same reset formula still appears three or more times in those beats.
findings.problem: The chapter leans on repeated reset constructions more than twice, especially 'He sat ...' and 'The wards were ...'. That repetition turns genuine awe into a detectable prose tic. Each renewed look at the tower state or the ward state should enter from a fresh angle, but these openings repeatedly announce the same beat before the sentence earns it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the paragraph-entry syntax at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:25, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:73, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:27, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:87, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_13.md:127. Convert at least half of these returns into different kinds of openings such as sensory intrusion, physical consequence, or perception already in motion so the prose stops resetting to the same declarative template.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised tower passage at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2653-2657 makes the outside feel indifferent and structurally incompatible without using water-table, groundwater, sea, or dam language. Fail if the paragraph still reads as another version of chapter 08's hydrological explanation.
findings.problem: Full-book recurring pattern: the outside/ward relation is rendered through hydrological metaphor in 5 chapters (chapters 08, 13, 16, 17, 18). In this chapter the 'water table rising' explanation repeats the same conceptual bridge the novel already used, so the tower revelation lands as a rephrasing of old language rather than a newly perceived terror. REPLACE this chapter's instance.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the explanatory run around snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2653, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2655, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2657 so it no longer uses water-table or groundwater imagery. Keep the indifference, incompatibility, and scale, but translate them through a different domain available on the tower scene itself: altitude, atmosphere, astronomy, stone erosion, or some other fresh concrete frame that Gavin could plausibly reach for in that moment.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central risk is tonal: it must sustain dark comedy, cosmic horror, and genuine emotional tenderness in the same narrative without any register canceling the others. Lean into the discomfort of juxtaposition. A scene where Gavin is mentally modeling ward-substrate decay while a classmate screams under the Cruciatus Curse is not a failure of empathy — it is the novel's core tension made visceral. The humor of the premise — that Voldemort's war is an irritating distraction from the real problem — must never be defused by making Gavin feel appropriately guilty at the right moments. He should feel guilty sometimes and not other times, and the reader should sit with the discomfort of a protagonist whose priorities have become genuinely alien. The cosmic material should be rendered with enough specificity that it is genuinely unsettling, not just philosophically interesting. Do not soften the ending: Gavin's refusal to intervene in the Battle of Hogwarts must be played straight, without a last-minute redemptive gesture or moral safety net.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in this novel is a particularly acute failure because the entire thematic engine runs on the difference between seeing something directly and not seeing it. Gavin's journey is toward unmediated perception. The prose must mirror that commitment. When the Carrows torture a student, render the torture. When Gavin is hit with the Cruciatus, render the pain in his body. When the cosmic threat presses against perception, render what it feels like — do not retreat to abstractions about 'something vast' or 'an incomprehensible presence.' Find the specific sensory detail that makes the incomprehensible land. Euphemism in a novel about the failure to see what is real is thematic self-sabotage.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, Babbling's academic register, or moments where a character is being pointedly formal. If a line reads stiffly, check contractions first.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter textures aggressively. A high-intensity chapter (Cruciatus detention, Duncan's torture) must be followed by a chapter with a different pressure profile — quieter, more internal, more comic, or more focused on intellectual work. No two consecutive chapters should share the same dominant emotional register, the same scene-structure (e.g., two chapters that each build to a confrontation), or the same pacing profile. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and the ratio of interiority to action. Some chapters should be mostly one extended scene; others should move across multiple locations and timeframes. The Christmas chapter and the work-routine chapters should breathe differently from the Carrow chapters and the battle chapter.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain, concrete, specific. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred over Latinate where both are available. Avoid poetic diction, literary allusion, and metaphors that call attention to their own cleverness. Metaphors should arise from Gavin's actual frame of reference: schoolwork, the castle, ordinary physical experience. When the First Art requires description, reach for precision over beauty — describe what it looks like, not what it means. The novel's most striking language should come from the collision between ordinary diction and extraordinary perception.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Gavin's perception throughout. The narrative voice has access to his thoughts, feelings, and sensory experience but not to anyone else's interiority. Other characters are known only through what Gavin observes. As the First Art restructures Gavin's cognition, the narrative's perceptual range should gradually expand — he begins noticing things no one else can see — but the voice remains grounded in his ordinary, slightly bewildered perspective. The narration should never become more articulate or literary than Gavin himself would be; the strangeness of what he perceives should press against the ordinariness of how he describes it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Default rhythm is medium-paced prose with sentences of mixed length — some short, some compound, none habitually long. During scenes of intellectual discovery, let sentences extend as Gavin's attention extends, then cut short when comprehension fails. During Carrow scenes, prose should tighten and become more physical. During quiet scenes, allow room for observation and sensory detail without rushing toward the next event. Do not develop a signature rhythm that persists across all chapters — the rhythm should be the scene's rhythm, not the author's.
